name: Ant Colony
description: Eusocial insect colony demonstrating decentralized intelligence through pheromone signaling, quorum sensing, and division of labor. No individual ant has a map of the colony's goals — coordination is entirely emergent.

scope.definition: Ratio of food energy retrieved to forager energy expended, measured across Argentine ant trails under optimal conditions.

evidence.source: Deneubourg et al. (1990), self-reinforcing trail formation studies; multiple ant genera confirmed.

scope.falsifiability: Foraging efficiency drops below 0.7 when pheromone gradients are disrupted or colony size falls below viable threshold.
scope.measurement_limits: Measured in laboratory corridors and semi-natural environments; extreme field conditions may vary.

scope.definition: Fraction of colony that must agree before collective action initiates (nest relocation, bridge formation). Below this fraction, the signal dissipates.

evidence.source: Pratt et al. (2002) nest emigration studies; Temnothorax genus.

scope.falsifiability: If a colony can be shown to act on signals from fewer than 5% of scouts, quorum sensing fails as model.
scope.measurement_limits: Threshold varies by species and colony health.

scope.definition: Colony recovery rate after 30% worker loss, measured as return to foraging baseline within 2 weeks.

evidence.source: Gordon (1999) long-term Pogonomyrmex colony studies; 25 years of observation.

scope.falsifiability: Colony fails to recover if queen is lost or brood is destroyed.
scope.measurement_limits: Resilience drops with repeated disturbances under 30-day intervals.

attributes.division_of_labor: Emergent role differentiation without central assignment: workers shift roles based on colony need and local pheromone density.
attributes.communication_mode: Pheromone gradient + antennal contact. No vocalizations. Signal degrades in ~10 minutes, preventing false trails from persisting.
attributes.pattern: decentralized emergence, positive feedback with natural decay

links.note: Colony tunnels often approximate hexagonal packing for structural efficiency.
Both use pheromone/waggle communication and quorum sensing for collective decision.
Both distribute information without central nodes; both show self-repairing network topology.
